rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative three-component cyclization of allylic alcohols, arylboronic acids, and dichalcogenides (RSeSeR/RSSR) to access chalcogenated cyclic boronic esters (1,3,2-dioxaborinanes) that are hydrolyzed to chalcogenated 1,3-diols.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation generates the active chalcogenating species: in pathway I, Br- is oxidized to Br+ (Br2) which reacts with PhSeSePh to give electrophilic PhSeBr; in pathway II, direct anodic SET oxidation of PhSeSePh gives a diselenide radical cation that fragments to PhSe• + PhSe+. Protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2.

key_intermediates.name: boronate ester I from condensation of cinnamyl alcohol and PhB(OH)2 (allyloxy-arylboronate)
seleniranium/β-selenocarbocation intermediate II on boronate scaffold (pathway I)
carbon-centered β-selenoalkyl radical III on boronate scaffold (pathway II)
β-selenoalkyl cation IV after anodic oxidation of III (pathway II)

electron_flow: Allylic alcohol and ArB(OH)2 condense (TFA) to an allyloxy boronate I; in parallel, the anode oxidizes either Br- (to Br+/Br2, which converts PhSeSePh to PhSeBr) or PhSeSePh directly to a radical cation that cleaves to PhSe• + PhSe+. The electrophilic/radical Se species adds to the tethered alkene of I to give a seleniranium (cationic, pathway I) or a C-radical that is anodically oxidized to a cation (pathway II); intramolecular trapping by the pendant B-OH oxygen closes the six-membered cyclic boronate 4a, while protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Yield of 3a/4a decreased to 20% with 2 equiv TEMPO, indicating radical involvement.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Yield dropped to 28% with 2 equiv BHT, consistent with radical intermediates.

probe_reagents.name: p-benzoquinone (BQ)
probe_reagents.observation: Yield dropped to 22% with 2 equiv BQ, supporting a radical pathway.

probe_reagents.name: PhSeBr (stoichiometric, no current)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction of cinnamyl alcohol + PhB(OH)2 + PhSeBr without electricity gave 4a in 78% yield, supporting PhSeBr as the active electrophile in pathway I.

notes: Two mechanisms are proposed and presented as co-existing: pathway I (Br+/PhSeBr-mediated electrophilic seleniranium, cation-initiated cyclization) is supported by the control experiment with stoichiometric PhSeBr giving 4a in 78% without current. Pathway II (direct anodic oxidation of PhSeSePh to a radical cation, generating PhSe• + PhSe+, radical addition then anodic oxidation to a cation — a radical-polar crossover) is supported by partial inhibition with TEMPO/BHT/BQ. The authors do not unambiguously favor one pathway over the other.
